spec_match.rationale: Uses if(supports(...)) per-property to gate corner-shape, text-box-trim, and container-type behind feature detection inline — exactly what the spec advertises as the alternative to @supports.

interactive.rationale: Stage paints live but probe shows zero buttons / inputs — the page only renders the result; no toggle to compare with/without the if(supports()) gate.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises per-property fallbacks for three modern features; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: a toggle to force-disable supports detection so authors can see the fallback path live.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the mandatory ChromeStatus feature 6313805904347136 link.

design_system.rationale: Raw hex literals appear inline (`#e8eeff`, `#edfdf0`, `#fde8e8`) for the demo cards. CLAUDE.md section 5 disallows raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Add a toggle to force the fallback path
openQuestions.detail: Today the page renders the supported branch on Chrome 137+ and authors never see the fallback. Add a toggle that swaps the if(supports(...)) for if(supports(fake-prop)) so the fallback paints in the live stage.

openQuestions.title: Replace inline hex with design system tokens
openQuestions.detail: The three card bgs (`#e8eeff`, `#edfdf0`, `#fde8e8`) should be page-local custom properties at the top, or design system tokens.

summary: Useful per-property progressive enhancement showcase, but it still needs a forced-fallback toggle and tokenized card colors.
